articles.title: Mīmāṃsā under the symbolism-shift reading
articles.subtitle: Pūrva-Mīmāṃsā as a coherent practice-philosophy whose externalized form is intact while the symbol-environment that activated it has changed.

articles.title: Vedānta as a realist tradition — a dialectical history in the schools' own voices
articles.subtitle: Pre-Śaṅkara through early-modern Vedānta as a chain of named polemics rather than a museum of labels.

articles.title: Śaṅkara — *adhyāsa*, *mithyā* without *asat*, the *līlā-kaivalyam* of the Self
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Brahmasūtra-bhāṣya*, *Upadeśasāhasrī*, and the major Upaniṣad commentaries.

articles.title: Rāmānuja — *saptavidhānupapatti*, *apṛthak-siddhi*, and the body of Brahman
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Śrī-bhāṣya* and *Vedārtha-saṃgraha*.

articles.title: Madhva — *pañcabheda*, *viśeṣa*, and the *māyāvāda* critique
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Brahmasūtra-bhāṣya*, *Anuvyākhyāna*, *Viṣṇu-tattva-vinirṇaya*, and *Māyāvāda-khaṇḍana*.

articles.title: Caitanya, Jīva Gosvāmī, Baladeva — *acintya-bhedābheda* as formal logic
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the Gauḍīya *Sandarbhas* and the *Govinda-bhāṣya*.

articles.title: Sri Aurobindo — triple-status Supermind, willful self-veiling, the gnostic being
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *The Life Divine*, *The Synthesis of Yoga*, *The Secret of the Veda*, and the *Record of Yoga*.

articles.title: Chaudhuri and Banerji — what is already in print since 1967
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through Haridas Chaudhuri's *Integral Yoga* and *Evolution of Integral Consciousness*, and Debashish Banerji's *Seven Quartets of Becoming* and the *Monsoon* essays.

articles.title: Krishnachandra Bhattacharyya — *The Subject as Freedom*, alternative absolutes, *Swaraj in Ideas*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *The Subject as Freedom*, *Studies in Vedantism*, *Studies in Philosophy*, and the 1928 "Swaraj in Ideas" lecture.

articles.title: Ramakrishna and Vivekananda — *vijñāna* doctrine, Bengali Vedānta, the Mother
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Kathāmṛta*, the *Complete Works* of Vivekananda, and the Holy Mother's letters.

articles.title: Swami Medhananda — *Vijñāna Vedānta* and Vivekananda's cosmopolitanism
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Infinite Paths to Infinite Reality* (OUP 2018) and *Swami Vivekananda's Vedāntic Cosmopolitanism* (OUP 2022), with the *Religions* / *Monist* / *PEW* essays.

articles.title: Reading Sāṃkhya with Anirban Bandyopadhyay — three-guṇa metaphysics and contemporary biophysics
articles.subtitle: Īśvarakṛṣṇa's *Sāṃkhya-Kārikā* with Gauḍapāda and Vācaspati Miśra, in counterpoint with Anirban Bandyopadhyay's Phase Prime Metric formalization of the three guṇas.

articles.title: Kāla-Cakra and nested topological clock-structures
articles.subtitle: Anirban Bandyopadhyay's polyatomic-time-crystal architecture against the classical Indic Kāla-Cakra: Bhāgavata cosmology, the four pralayas, the *Kālacakra-Tantra*.

articles.title: Deleuze — univocity of being, the rhizomatic real, and the fold
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in original French with English translation through *Différence et Répétition*, *Logique du sens*, *Mille Plateaux*, *Le Pli*, and *Spinoza et le problème de l'expression*.

articles.title: Nietzsche — perspectivism, eternal return, the genealogy of values
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Die Geburt der Tragödie*, *Zur Genealogie der Moral*, *Also sprach Zarathustra*, *Jenseits von Gut und Böse*, and the *Nachlass*.

articles.title: Hegel — *Aufhebung*, the speculative proposition, the labor of the negative
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Phänomenologie des Geistes*, *Wissenschaft der Logik*, and *Encyclopädie* in German with English alongside.

articles.title: Spinoza — substance, attribute, mode; *intellectus infinitus* and the *amor Dei intellectualis*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in Latin with English translation through the *Ethica*, *Tractatus de Intellectus Emendatione*, and *Korte Verhandeling*.

articles.title: K. C. Bhattacharyya, the Kantian critique, and the Jaina-realist alternative to Hegelian sublation
articles.subtitle: KCB against Hegelian totalization, the limits of evolutionary metaphysics, and the defense of being and becoming as orthogonal registers.

articles.title: Leibniz — the monad as perspective, pre-established harmony, the aperspectival God
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through the *Monadologie*, *Discours de métaphysique*, *Nouveaux Essais*, and the late correspondence with Clarke and Des Bosses.

articles.title: Heidegger — *Sein und Zeit*, *Ereignis*, the clearing
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in German with English alongside through *Sein und Zeit*, the *Wegmarken* essays, *Identität und Differenz*, the *Beiträge*, and *Gelassenheit*.

articles.title: Husserl — intentionality, the *Lebenswelt*, transcendental constitution
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in German through the *Logische Untersuchungen*, *Ideen*, *Cartesianische Meditationen*, and *Krisis*.

articles.title: Adorno — *Negative Dialektik*, identity-thinking as violence, the *Vorrang des Objekts*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in German through *Negative Dialektik*, *Minima Moralia*, *Dialektik der Aufklärung*, and *Jargon der Eigentlichkeit*.

articles.title: Bergson — *durée*, the *élan vital*, intuition as method
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in French through *Essai sur les données immédiates*, *Matière et mémoire*, *L'évolution créatrice*, and *Les deux sources*.

articles.title: Whitehead — process, prehension, the dipolar God
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Process and Reality*, *Adventures of Ideas*, *Modes of Thought*, and *Religion in the Making*.

articles.title: Prigogine — irreversibility, dissipative structures, the arrow of time
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *La nouvelle alliance*, *From Being to Becoming*, *The End of Certainty*, and the technical papers on far-from-equilibrium thermodynamics.

articles.title: Derrida — *différance*, trace, deconstruction
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *De la grammatologie*, *L'écriture et la différence*, *Marges de la philosophie*, and *La voix et le phénomène*.

articles.title: Foucault — *épistémè*, power-knowledge, the care of the self
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in French following Foucault's three modes of objectification: archaeological *savoirs*, the genealogy of power, the ethical axis.

articles.title: Levinas — the face of the Other, *Totalité et Infini*, *Autrement qu'être*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session in French through *De l'existence à l'existant*, *Totalité et infini*, *Autrement qu'être*, and the *Lectures talmudiques*.

articles.title: McGilchrist — the hemispheric thesis, *non-duality of duality and non-duality*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *The Master and His Emissary* (2009) and *The Matter with Things* (2021).

articles.title: Gebser — *Ursprung und Gegenwart*, the aperspectival-integral mutation, *Diaphaneität*
articles.subtitle: A reading-session through *Ursprung und Gegenwart* and the Barstad–Mickunas English (Ohio UP 1985), recovering the consciousness-mutation thesis and Gebser's 1966 equation of the integral with Aurobindo's supramental.

articles.title: Hegel's Preface to the *Phänomenologie* — and the Vedāntic parallels
articles.subtitle: *Substanz als Subjekt*, *Aufhebung*, the speculative proposition placed against Bhartṛprapañca, Madhva, Vyāsatīrtha, Jīva Gosvāmī, Vidyāraṇya.

articles.title: Primitive graph — a node-and-edge grammar for comparative philosophy
articles.subtitle: A register-disciplined graph of primitives, commitments, dependencies, and critique edges across Vedāntic and comparator positions.

articles.title: An analytic-primitive model of Vedāntic and adjacent positions
articles.subtitle: Nine structural primitives — substrate-independence, mode-status, identity-relation, causation, locus-of-avidyā, *bheda*-count, epistemic-authority, *mokṣa*-state, *bhakti*-role — and the derivation table per school.
articles.superseded_note: Replaced by the node-and-edge primitive graph, which splits primitive nodes from commitment and critique edges and adds register discipline.

articles.title: The comparative-claims framework — methodology
articles.subtitle: A four-verdict schema for cross-school comparison: shared-presupposition, parallel-structure, terminological-equivalence, genuine-disagreement, contested.
articles.superseded_note: Its four-verdict schema now lives inside the primitive graph as a classification of critique and subsumption edges.

articles.title: Madhva's *Brahma-Sūtra-Bhāṣya* with Tattva-Prakāśikā, Tātparya-Candrikā, Nyāya-Sudhā, Parimaḷa
articles.subtitle: A walk-through across three commentarial layers covering BS 1.1–1.2 — the *bheda-pañcaka*, the realism-recovery move on BS 1.1.1, the constructive thesis that *mithyā ≠ asat*.

articles.title: Madhva's *Bhāgavata-Tātparya-Nirṇaya*
articles.subtitle: Madhva's settling-of-purport reading of the *Bhāgavata Purāṇa* — the *bhakti* axis of Tattvavāda, the gradation of *jīva*s, the locus of Viṣṇu's primacy in scripture.

articles.title: Madhva's *Māyāvāda-Khaṇḍana*
articles.subtitle: The polemical demolition of Advaitic *māyāvāda* — Madhva's argument that *mithyā* construed as ontological non-existence is incoherent, and the constructive corollary for the realism-recovery trajectory.

articles.title: Nimbārka's *Vedānta-Pārijāta-Saurabha* with Śrīnivāsa's *Vedānta-Kaustubha*
articles.subtitle: Dvaitādvaita's parallel-realism reading of the Brahma-Sūtras — *bheda* and *abheda* as equiprimordial, the cit–acit–Brahman triad, where Nimbārka sits between Rāmānuja and Madhva.

articles.title: Vallabha's *Aṇu-Bhāṣya* on the Brahma-Sūtras
articles.subtitle: Śuddhādvaita as a third realism-Vedānta — the world as Brahman's *āvirbhāva–tirobhāva*, the rejection of *pariṇāma* and *vivarta*, the *bhakti*-axis of pure non-duality.

articles.title: Vedānta-Deśika's *Pāñcarātra-Rakṣā*
articles.subtitle: Deśika's defense of the scriptural authority of the Pāñcarātra against Mīmāṃsaka and Smārta attack — the tantric-Vaiṣṇava locus of Viśiṣṭādvaita's ritual ontology.

articles.title: Rāghavendra's *Nyāya-Sudhā-Parimaḷa* (4 vols)
articles.subtitle: Rāghavendra's gloss on Jayatīrtha's *Nyāya-Sudhā* on Madhva's *Anuvyākhyāna* — the late-Mādhva polemic apparatus deployed across all four *adhyāya*s of the Brahma-Sūtra.

articles.title: Rāghavendra's *Tantra-Dīpikā*
articles.subtitle: Rāghavendra's commentary on Madhva's *Tantra-Sāra-Saṅgraha* — the Mādhva ritual-philosophical interface, mantra ontology, the *bhakti*–*jñāna* integration.

articles.title: Vidyāraṇya's *Vivaraṇa-Prameya-Saṅgraha*
articles.subtitle: The Vivaraṇa-school digest — *śravaṇa* / *manana* / *nididhyāsana*, the *mānasī-vṛtti* whose object is *pratyag-brahmaikya*, and the locus of Brahman as both *āśraya* and *viṣaya* of avidyā.

articles.title: Vijñānabhikṣu's *Vijñānāmṛta-Bhāṣya* on the Brahma-Sūtras
articles.subtitle: Vijñānabhikṣu's *avibhāgādvaita* — non-duality-by-non-difference rather than identity, the synthesis of Sāṃkhya and Vedānta, and the late-medieval reframing of *jīva*–Brahman relation.

articles.title: Vijñānabhikṣu's *Yoga-Vārttika*
articles.subtitle: Vijñānabhikṣu's gloss on Vyāsa's *Yoga-Bhāṣya* — Pātañjala Yoga read into Vedāntic ontology, the place of Īśvara, the *kleśa*–*vāsanā* architecture.

articles.title: Yāmuna's *Āgama-Prāmāṇya*
articles.subtitle: Yāmuna's defense of the Pāñcarātra Āgama as scripturally authoritative — the founding text of the Viśiṣṭādvaita line, refuting the Mīmāṃsaka rejection of non-Vedic ritual literature.

articles.title: Madhusūdana Sarasvatī's *Bhakti-Rasāyana*
articles.subtitle: Madhusūdana's reconciliation of *bhakti* with Advaitic non-dualism — *bhakti* as a *rasa* and a distinct *puruṣārtha*, the bridge from *Advaita-Siddhi* to a non-dual devotionalism.

articles.title: Rāmānuja's *Śrī-Bhāṣya*
articles.subtitle: The full Viśiṣṭādvaita reading of the Brahma-Sūtras — the *mahā-pūrva-pakṣa* against Advaita on BS 1.1.1, the *aprākṛta-śarīraka* construal of *jīva*–Brahman, qualified non-duality.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā worked — symbol-environment thesis (working draft, superseded)
articles.subtitle: Working investigation that reinstated the symbolism-shift thesis. Superseded by the polished Perspectives article 'Mīmāṃsā under the symbolism-shift reading'.
articles.superseded_note: v4's content was lifted into the polished Perspectives article 'Mīmāṃsā under the symbolism-shift reading'. Kept on disk for the historical record of the v1→v2→v3→v4 trajectory.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as a coherent practice-philosophy — Clooney's reading (superseded)
articles.subtitle: Replaced v2's diagnostic-decline framing. Superseded by v4 (and now by the polished Perspectives article).
articles.superseded_note: v3 over-corrected v2's diagnostic-decline framing by retracting the symbolism-shift thesis entirely. v4 reinstated it; the polished Perspectives article supersedes both.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as externalized yogic process — re-investigation (superseded)
articles.subtitle: Re-do with stronger verdict, drawing on Clooney 2022. Introduced a diagnostic-decline framing later retracted. Superseded.

articles.title: Mīmāṃsā as externalized yogic process — first investigation (superseded)
articles.subtitle: First-pass investigation. Superseded.

articles.title: Schelling — nature, freedom, the dark ground, the unprethinkable being
articles.subtitle: A lecture-depth reading through the Naturphilosophie, the *System*, *Bruno*, the *Freiheitsschrift*, *Weltalter*, and the late Munich and Berlin lectures.

articles.title: Late Plato — the unfinished metaphysics
articles.subtitle: A reader on the late dialogues, read through the continuity between early aporia and the guarded metaphysical speech of the late corpus.

articles.title: Wittgenstein — language-games, hinges, and the limits of the sayable
articles.subtitle: A reader's document on the `Tractatus`, `Philosophical Investigations`, and `On Certainty`, written around the problem of language-games, hinges, and the limits of saying.
